title: Cannot get started contributing on MacOS
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind feature

I guess it could be either, depending on which way you look at it.

**Description**

I'm running MacOS and want to contribute to Podman. I'm having an issue trying to even build the podman CLI and can't help contribute.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Run MacOS
2. All documentation on getting started is geared towards Linux, so I'm not sure what prereqs are relevant, but I've made a best-effort attempt (although my best effort is probably not great)
3. run `make binaries`:

**Describe the results you received:**
```> make binaries
Makefile:134: invalid `override' directive
Podman is being compiled without the systemd build tag.         Install libsystemd on Ubuntu or systemd-devel on rpm based      distro for journald support.
CGO_ENABLED=0 GOOS=darwin GOARCH=arm64 go build \
                 \
                -ldflags '-X github.com/containers/podman/v4/libpod/define.gitCommit=6826bf427069ae2e8ac212802e8b60a3f433867c -X github.com/containers/podman/v4/libpod/define.buildInfo=1664995096 -X github.com/containers/podman/v4/libpod/config._installPrefix=/usr/local -X github.com/containers/podman/v4/libpod/config._etcDir=/usr/local/etc -X github.com/containers/common/pkg/config.additionalHelperBinariesDir= ' \
                -tags " exclude_graphdriver_btrfs btrfs_noversion    exclude_graphdriver_devicemapper seccomp" \
                -o bin/podman ./cmd/podman
package github.com/containers/podman/v4/cmd/podman
        imports github.com/containers/podman/v4/cmd/podman/common
        imports github.com/containers/podman/v4/cmd/podman/registry
        imports github.com/containers/podman/v4/pkg/domain/infra
        imports github.com/containers/podman/v4/libpod
        imports github.com/containers/common/libnetwork/cni: build constraints exclude all Go files in /Users/kbater/Projects/containers/podman/vendor/github.com/containers/common/libnetwork/cni
package github.com/containers/podman/v4/cmd/podman
        imports github.com/containers/podman/v4/cmd/podman/common
        imports github.com/containers/podman/v4/cmd/podman/registry
        imports github.com/containers/podman/v4/pkg/domain/infra
        imports github.com/containers/podman/v4/libpod
        imports github.com/containers/common/libnetwork/network: build constraints exclude all Go files in /Users/kbater/Projects/containers/podman/vendor/github.com/containers/common/libnetwork/network
make: *** [bin/podman] Error 1```

**Describe the results you expected:**
go builds the podman binary into `bin/podman`

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**
Running this on MacOS Monterrey, 12.6, M1Pro 32GB RAM

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
> podman version
Client:       Podman Engine
Version:      4.2.1
API Version:  4.2.1
Go Version:   go1.18.6

```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman` or `brew info podman`):**

```
==> podman: stable 4.2.1 (bottled), HEAD

```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md)**

Yes

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
Physical

I'd love to know where I can help get started with the project, I'm eager to help bring this to MacOS.

comments.body:
hello @iamkirkbater ... you are correct; most things are geared towards starting on Linux and contributing from Linux.  But fear not, there are options!

the first thing to know is that on darwin, try make podman-remote.  this will build the native macos "remote" client, which is the client for Macs.  In this case, consider the client to be the "front" end.  The "backend" runs on Linux and only on Linux.  So if this is the case, you will need to create a VM of your choice and run Linux there to interact with it.

we can probably be more realtime about helping you if you are able to join us on irc -> https://podman.io/community/#slack-irc-matrix-and-discord

I'm going to convert this to a discussion as it is a perfect fit for that.
